title: School enrollment, secondary (% gross)
definition: Gross enrollment ratio is the ratio of total enrollment, regardless of age, to the population of the age group that officially corresponds to the level of education shown. Secondary education completes the provision of basic education that began at the primary level, and aims at laying the foundations for lifelong learning and human development, by offering more subject- or skill-oriented instruction using more specialized teachers.

Detailed documentation is available at: https://api.uis.unesco.org/api/public/documentation/, publisher: UNESCO Institute for Statistics (UIS), type: Bulk file (csv), date accessed: 2026-03-19, date published: 2026-02

editorial.patternNote: The darkest countries include Belgium, Finland, Netherlands, while the lightest include Somalia, Fed. Rep., Niger, Chad; that contrast is the fastest way to read the secondary enrollment pattern.
editorial.whyItMatters: Secondary enrollment often separates countries that look similar on basic schooling.

bestProbeCountries.reason: High anchor: this country sits near the darkest end of the map.
Low anchor: this country clarifies the lightest end of the scale.
Middle check: this country helps separate a true pattern from one-off outliers.

commonConfusions.reason: Particulate emission damage can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.
Health spending per person can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.

editorial.difficultyReason: Standard difficulty: the map has readable regional structure, but the best answer still depends on checking the unit and country anchors.
regionalSignals: Education indicators can be shaped by enrollment systems and data coverage.
Compare youth-heavy countries with higher-income service economies.

reviewNotes: Stronger education signal than primary enrollment.
Coverage is just adequate, so keep missing-data caveat visible.
